sanskrit: त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्जुन । निर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान् ॥
hindiTranslation: वेद तीनों गुणोंके कार्यका ही वर्णन करनेवाले हैं हे अर्जुन तू तीनों गुणोंसे रहित हो जा, निर्द्वन्द्व हो जा, निरन्तर नित्य वस्तु परमात्मा में स्थित हो जा, योगक्षेमकी चाहना भी मत रख और परमात्मपरायण हो जा ॥
englishTranslation: The Vedas deal with the three qualities. Arjuna, rise beyond the three qualities, beyond opposites, fixed in lasting reality, free from concern for gain and security, and established in the true self.
essence: Freedom begins when gain and loss stop defining you.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The sacred texts describe the world of changing forces, but Krishna tells Arjuna to rise above them. He should stop chasing comfort and security, and stay rooted in what is unchanging.
simpleInsight: There is relief in not being ruled by constant wanting, fear, or comparison.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen before the war. Krishna now moves from duty to inner freedom: the Vedas describe worldly patterns, but Arjuna must stand beyond them, unshaken by gain, loss, or duality.
modernRelevance: You are refreshing your bank app for the tenth time, waiting for reassurance. The verse says the deeper life begins when you stop living by gain, fear, and the next update.
translationLiteral: त्रैगुण्यविषया (concerned with the three qualities) / वेदा (the Vedas) / निस्त्रैगुण्यो (one beyond the three qualities) / भव (be) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / निर्द्वन्द्वो (free from opposites) / नित्यसत्त्वस्थो (steadfast in the ever-real) / निर्योगक्षेमः (free from concern for acquisition and preservation) / आत्मवान् (established in the true self)
insight: It shifts attention from managing outcomes to resting in what does not change.

quoteVariants.text: Rise beyond the three qualities. Stand where gain and loss no longer rule you.
Beyond the three qualities. Beyond gain and loss.
The Vedas point here, but you must go beyond.
Go beyond opposites. Rest in what lasts.
